title: Pancakes

ingredients.title: For the Fish
items: 200 g (1 ½ cups) all-purpose flour, sifted
300 ml (1 ¼ cups) buttermilk, room temperature
50 g (¼ cup) unsalted butter, melted and slightly cooled
Neutral oil or clarified butter, for cooking

ingredients.title: Garnishes (Restaurant Style)
items: Optional: candied nuts or a drizzle of fruit coulis for plating

instruction.title: Dry Mix
steps: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt until evenly combined.
Sifting ensures lightness and prevents lumps.

instruction.title: Wet Mix
steps: In a separate bowl, whisk together buttermilk, eggs, melted butter, and vanilla until smooth.

instruction.title: Combine
steps: Gently pour the wet ingredients into the dry.
Use a spatula to fold just until combined — do not overmix. A few small lumps are ideal for fluffy pancakes.
Rest the batter for 5–10 minutes (helps gluten relax and makes pancakes tender).

instruction.title: Cooking
steps: Heat a nonstick pan or griddle over medium heat; lightly grease with neutral oil or clarified butter.
Pour about ¼ cup batter per pancake.
Cook until bubbles form on the surface and edges look set (2–3 minutes). Flip and cook another 1–2 minutes until golden brown.
Keep warm on a tray in a 90°C (200°F) oven if making multiple batches.

instruction.title: Plating (Restaurant Quality)
steps: Stack 3 pancakes neatly in the center of a warmed plate.
Drizzle with warm maple syrup in a slow ribbon pour.
Garnish with a quenelle of whipped cream or mascarpone on the side.
Add a cluster of fresh berries for color contrast.
Lightly dust with powdered sugar for elegance.
For fine dining presentation: add a swipe of berry coulis or a few candied nuts.

chefTips: For extra fluffiness, separate the eggs — whisk the egg whites to soft peaks and fold gently into the batter at the end. This creates an airy, soufflé-like texture perfect for high-end service.
